{{Quote|He was a healer. Wise. Strong. A man who commanded respect.|The Huntress|<ref name="doe">''[[Darth Bane: Dynasty of Evil]]''</ref>}}
'''Caleb''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Force healing/Legends|healer]] who resided on the abandoned [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Ambria/Legends|Ambria]]. He was [[Confrontation on Ambria|killed]] by [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] [[Darth Zannah/Legends|Zannah]] in [[990 BBY]].

==Biography==
===Meeting Darth Bane===
Caleb lived during the last days of the [[New Sith Wars]], but refused to be a party to the wars, and sought the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ambria/Legends|Ambria]] as a refuge. He turned away both [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], seeing himself as a neutral party and a healer to the common people.<ref name="Path of Destruction" />

His rigid policy of neutrality was challenged when the exiled [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]] came to [[Caleb's hut|his home]] in [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]], seeking a cure for the [[Synox/Legends|synox]] with which his former lover [[Githany]] had poisoned him. Caleb refused to treat him at first, going so far as to thrust his hand into a cauldron of boiling liquid to demonstrate his impartiality to pain. This implied that he would be immune to any [[Torture/Legends|torture]] that the Sith Lord had planned; they would have no power over him since he accepted his fate. The impossibility of scaring the healer frustrated Bane, however, Bane sensed the nearby presence of [[Serra|Caleb's daughter]]. After Bane threatened to end her life, Caleb agreed to cure him. Sensing that Caleb could be useful in the future, Bane allowed him to live.<ref name="Path of Destruction" />

[[File:CalebSerraBane-JediVsSith1.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Darth Bane threatens Caleb by means of his daughter]]

===Death===
In [[990 BBY]], when [[Darth Zannah/Legends|Darth Zannah]] came looking for Caleb, he refused to heal Darth Bane, who had been mortally injured. [[Darovit]] made a deal with Caleb to heal Bane, as long as Zannah turned herself and Bane in to the Jedi and deactivated their ship, the [[Loranda (starship)|''Loranda'']]. At last Zannah complied and after Caleb healed Bane and removed the [[orbalisk]]s, he made sure Zannah sent out a message to the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] that there was a Sith Lord on Ambria, waiting to be arrested. Zannah, however, wished to save Bane and preserve the [[Order of the Sith Lords]], therefore she [[Death/Legends|killed]] Caleb and [[Summon fear|drove Darovit insane]] with [[Sith alchemy/Legends|her sorcery]]. Later, she hid Bane and herself in an underground hiding spot of Caleb's, [[Spell of concealment|cloaking]] their presences in [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. The Jedi arrived and found Caleb's body chopped up outside of his shack. As they neared, Darovit [[Confrontation on Ambria|attacked them]] and was killed almost instantly. The Jedi took all the pieces of Caleb's corpse back to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] to give him a proper funeral.<ref name="Rule of Two" />

===Legacy===
Once returned to the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]], the Jedi created a memorial to those Jedi who were responsible for the destruction of the Sith. All five of the Jedi's [[Lightsaber hilt/Legends|lightsaber hilts]] were set into a large white stone block in the gardens around the base of the [[Tower of First Knowledge/Legends|Tower of First Knowledge]]. In honor to his sacrifice for the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]], Caleb's name was inscribed in the block. In [[980 BBY]], Caleb's daughter, who had become [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] Serra of [[Doan]], saw the name while visiting the Temple to negotiate on behalf of her planet. Upon learning of her father's death, she ordered her [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]], [[Lucia (soldier)|Lucia]] to contact the [[Darth Cognus|Huntress]] to [[Skirmish in Darth Bane's mansion|capture]] Bane, who she believed had killed her father.<ref name="doe" />

==Skills and abilities==
Caleb's healing abilities stemmed from the Force but it was not like that of the Jedi or Sith; his power came directly from the world around him, the natural power of the [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]] and [[Forest/Legends|forests]]. Using the knowledge found in the [[Jedi Archives/Legends|Jedi Archives]] by Zannah, he was successfully able to remove Bane's [[orbalisk armor]] and save his life.<ref name="Rule of Two" />
